data.title: Uncertainty on revenue growth stalls at above prepandemic level - Atlanta Fed
data.description:
Business uncertainty about revenue growth (smoothed) for the four quarters ahead held at 3.80% in November, compared with 3.81% in October, according to the Atlanta Fed's Survey of Business Uncertainty released on Wednesday.

Businesses remain more uncertain about future sales growth than before the pandemic, the Atlanta Fed said.

Business uncertainty about employment growth (smoothed) for four quarters ahead stood at 4.35%  in November vs. 4.30% in October.

Firms surveyed reported that ~70% of their workforce is fully in person, with the rest working at least one remote day. The responses showed that most executives believe their employees are more productive in person than they are remote. However, executives at businesses which are completely remote say the opposite, the Atlanta Fed said.

data.title: Mixed signals as S&P 500’s December strength weakens in recent decade
data.description:
December’s reputation as a strong month for equities looks less straightforward when viewed through different seasonality lenses. 

Over the longer term, December stands out as one of the strongest performers for the S&P 500 (SP500), along with months like April, July and November, according to data compiled by TradeThatSwing. 

The final week of December is particularly of interest, as that is when the Santa Claus rally comes into effect. The phenomenon is a seven-session stretch covering the last five trading days of the year and the first two of January, during which stocks have traditionally shown a tendency to rally. 

However, December being a strong month might come into some contention, as when the window narrows to the past decade, the picture changes. 

In the 10-year seasonality chart, December slips into the lower tier, emerging as one of the weaker months alongside September, according to TradeThatSwing.

TradeThatSwing added that the SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) was used to generate the seasonality figures.

Here is a chart to show the S&P 500's (SP500) performance in the last 10 years, made by TradeThatSwing. To view more such charts, click here. 

 

 

Thus, while December's full-month average remains positive in the long view, the path through the month has become a bit more shaky. Early-month softness appears more often in the shorter-term pattern. 

Since the start of the year, the S&P 500 (SP500) was up 15%.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500: (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Left off the menu: Restaurant stocks have missed out on the AI-fueled rally
data.description:
Restaurant stocks have lagged broader retail this year by a significant margin, primarily because traffic and same-store sales have decelerated while costs for labor, food, rent, and interest remain at elevated levels to pressure margins. At the same time, consumers have shifted toward value formats and at-home consumption due to affordability issues, so many restaurant concepts look more cyclical to investors and less defensible than other retail categories.

The AI trade is also less relevant for restaurant chains in comparison to other retail categories that have seen an immediate boost in e-commerce conversions and efficient benefits in operations and marketing that have already had a profound impact on quarterly earnings and guidance updates. While restaurant companies are doing what they can to leverage generative AI, the story of its upside has been less appetizing to analysts and investors. 

The notable year-to-date decliners in the restaurant sector include Sweetgreen (SG) -78%, (GENK) -66%, (RICK) -57%, CAVA Group (CAVA) -55%, Jack in the Box (JACK) -52%, Wendy's (WEN) -48%, Portillo's (PTLO) -45%, Cracker Barrel (CBRL) -43%, Chipotle (CMG) -43%, Bloomin' Brands (BLM) -42%, Shake Shack (SHAK) -32%, and Red Robin Gourmet Burgers (RRGB) -22%.

Starbucks (SBUX) and Domino's Pizza (DPZ) are also in negative territory for the year. Bucking the general downtrend, restaurant operators Brinker International (EAT) +16.6%, Yum! Brands (YUM) +14.8%, Dutch Bros (BROS) +13.8%, and McDonald's (MCD) +7.4% have outperformed.

The Dow Jones US Restaurants & Bars Index (DJUSRU) is down 3.2% for the year vs. the +15.8% return for the S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Wall Street rises on fresh economic signals ahead of Thanksgiving
data.description:
Wall Street watched stocks climb higher on Wednesday, with traders and investors digesting new economic data amid ongoing rate-cut speculation.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) which rallied north of 650 points on Tuesday was last higher by 0.6%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) pushed up by 0.6% and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) advanced by 0.7%. 

Looking at the S&P 500 by sector, nine of the 11 segments are trending higher, with Info Tech leading the gains. On the downside, Communication Services has been the weakest performing area so far. 

Treasury yields advanced as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved higher by 2 basis points to 4.01% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) rose 3 basis points to 3.48%. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims surprised lower in the week ended November 22. Durable goods orders slowed in September, as expected. 

“Stocks are celebrating the likelihood of a December rate cut, as it's becoming clear that the labor market is the priority for the Federal Reserve, and another rate cut would help to protect against any further cooling of the job market,” Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated. 

“It's becoming more clear that the stock market declines seen over most of November were simply a pullback after an extremely strong run in October and September,” Stanley added. 

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Robinhood Markets (HOOD) tracked higher by 8.2% on the day while shares of Zscaler (ZS) dropped 11.4%.

For reference, markets will be closed on November 27, Thursday, for the Thanksgiving holiday.

  • Don't Buy This Dip: It's Black Friday In The Equity Market
  • AI Spending War And AI Debt Pile-Up Could Squeeze Share Buybacks

data.title: HSBC predicts S&P 500 to reach 7,500 by 2026 year-end
data.description:
HSBC has set a target of 7,500 for the S&P 500 (SP500) by the end of 2026, projecting another year of double-digit gains driven primarily by a booming AI investment cycle, according to their market outlook.

The EPS growth forecast is at 12% and supported by “macro stability, some easing of policy uncertainty, and an AI investment boom,” Nicole Inui, head of equity strategy for the Americas at HSBC, said.

The bank expects a “two-speed economy” in 2026, with AI strength counterbalancing a cautious U.S. consumer, according to the outlook.

HSBC also forecasts GDP growth of 1.7% in 2026, slightly below the consensus expectation of 1.8%, while projecting inflation to rise to 3% above the Fed’s 2% target.

“We see 2026 dominated by similar themes as 2025, including the AI investment-led boom, a bifurcated consumer, and policy uncertainty,” Inui said.

AI capital expenditure will continue to dominate economic activity as “the AI arms race intensifies,” with hyperscalers expected to spend upwards of half a trillion dollars in 2026, the bank predicts.

This spending will trickle down to other sectors such as utilities (XLU) and industrials (XLI), while potentially crowding out capital and labor supply elsewhere.

“Technology (XLK) accounted for circa 50% of incremental U.S. dollar (DXY) spend in 2025, followed by utilities (XLU),” strategists said.

In addition, the bifurcation between high and low-income consumers will widen further in 2026, HSBC warned, citing multiple factors. “The absence of Fed rate cuts, sticky inflation, a wobbly labor market, and fading/uncertain fiscal support are all headwinds for the consumer,” Inui explained, adding that disruptions in benefits programs like SNAP have affected 12% of U.S. households.

HSBC’s S&P 500 (SP500) target is based on expected earnings per share of $300, representing 12% year-over-year growth, slightly below the consensus estimate of $305.

“Given these assumptions, we take a trailing 12-month multiple of 25x (YTD average) to arrive at our end-2026 S&P target of 7,500,” Inui concluded, while cautioning that key risks include labor market deterioration, rising inflation, and escalation in trade policy uncertainty.

data.title: Orion Properties says Kawa's director nominations are invalid
data.description:
Orion Properties (ONL) said on Wednesday that the notice submitted by The Kawa Fund Limited, which purported to nominate five candidates for Orion's board, is invalid.

Orion's board unanimously determined that the submission didn't meet several requirements in Orion's bylaws, including that Kawa be a record holder of the company's common stock. The fund failed to provide proof that it's an eligible stockholder, the ONL board said.

"In addition, Kawa’s nomination notice does not comply with several other requirements of Orion’s bylaws, including a lack of discussion of Kawa’s plans for the company beyond a passing reference to Kawa’s two prior proposals to acquire all of the outstanding shares of Orion’s common stock," the company said.

Orion (ONL) will announce the date of its 2026 annual meeting and file proxy materials with the SEC "in due course," it said.

Orion (ONL) stock rose 4.6% in morning trading.

data.title: Gaotu Techedu outlines 17%-19% Q4 revenue growth target as AI integration and offline expansion drive results
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Gaotu Techedu Inc. (GOTU) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Larry Chen stated that the company's "user-centric approach continues to drive progress in our high-quality growth strategy" and highlighted the integration of AI across teaching services and operations, resulting in efficiency gains and a clearer path to profitability. He reported, "In the third quarter, we delivered another solid set of financial results. Revenue grew by 30.7% year-over-year to nearly RMB 1.6 billion, while on a non-GAAP basis, both loss from operations and net loss narrowed significantly by 64.6% and 69.9%, respectively."
  • Chen announced the completion of the initial share repurchase program, which was expanded to $80 million in 2023, and confirmed that the newly approved $100 million program has commenced. He emphasized, "We will continue to execute the share repurchase program in a prudent and disciplined manner while safeguarding operational and financial health."
  • Chen revealed that for the first time, revenue contribution from offline learning services exceeded 10% of total revenues, marking a milestone in the company's omni-channel strategy.
  • CFO Nan Shen stated, "In the third quarter, net revenues continued to grow and operating efficiency maintained a healthy trajectory, demonstrating that our investments in resource allocation optimization, cost structure refinement and AI-driven process transformation are steadily translating into structural improvements in profitability."

Outlook

  • CFO Nan Shen provided fourth-quarter guidance, stating, "Based on our current estimates, total net revenue for the fourth quarter of 2025 are expected to be between RMB 1,628 million and RMB 1,648 million, representing an increase of 17.2% to 18.7% on a year-over-year basis."
  • In the Q&A, Shen said, "In 2026, we expect our growth trajectory to become more balanced with profitability as the major focus, driving the execution of our overall strategy. So which means in 2026, profitability will play the most important role."
  • Shen projected "approximately 15% year-over-year revenue growth in 2026" and ongoing efforts towards "sustainable net profitability in 2026."

Financial Results

  • Revenue for the quarter reached nearly RMB 1.6 billion, an increase of 30.7% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit was over RMB 1.0 billion with a gross margin of 66.1%.
  • Cost of revenue was RMB 535.5 million.
  • Total operating expenses decreased 3.7% year-over-year to approximately RMB 1.2 billion. Selling expenses were RMB 873.4 million, R&D expenses RMB 162.9 million, and G&A expenses RMB 185.2 million.
  • Loss from operations was RMB 178 million, with an operating loss margin of 11.3%. Net loss was RMB 147.1 million, and net loss margin was 9.3%.
  • Deferred revenue rose to nearly RMB 1.8 billion, up 23.2% year-over-year.
  • As of September 30, 2025, cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash, and investments totaled over RMB 3.0 billion.
  • As of November 25, 2025, the company repurchased approximately 27.5 million ADS for nearly RMB 619 million.

Q&A

  • Crystal Li, China Merchants Securities: "Could you give us some color on your 2026 top line growth and your expectations on each business line? And in terms of the bottom line, how is your plan? Maybe could you share more about your plan to balance your growth and loss reduction?" CFO Nan Shen: "Looking back at 2024 and 2025, our revenue grew 53.8% year-over-year in 2024. And based on our latest guidance, we expect close to 35% year-over-year growth of our top line in 2025. ... In 2026, we expect our growth trajectory to become more balanced with profitability as the major focus, driving the execution of our overall strategy. ... we anticipate approximately 15% year-over-year revenue growth in 2026."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a positive tone, congratulating management and focusing on growth and profitability outlooks, with no apparent skepticism or pressing concerns.
  • Management's tone was confident and optimistic in prepared remarks, emphasizing measurable improvements and long-term value creation. During the Q&A, management reiterated focus on profitability and balanced growth, showing consistency with their prepared remarks.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment from both analysts and management remains positive, with a continued emphasis on growth and operational improvement.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth moderated from 37.6% in Q2 to 30.7% in Q3, while operating loss and net loss both narrowed further.
  • Guidance for Q4 targets 17.2% to 18.7% revenue growth, lower than Q3 but suggesting ongoing expansion.
  • Deferred revenue grew 23.2% year-over-year in Q3, slightly below the 38.9% growth in Q2.
  • Management's tone is consistently confident, maintaining focus on AI-driven operational improvements, offline expansion, and shareholder value creation. Analysts' focus shifted from offline growth and summer enrollment (Q2) to long-term profitability and growth balance (Q3).

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited "known or unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors" that may affect results, and noted that guidance contains forward-looking statements involving risks beyond their control.
  • No specific new risks or challenges were highlighted beyond existing macro and operational uncertainties.

Final Takeaway

Gaotu Techedu emphasized its continued momentum in revenue growth, operational efficiency, and AI integration, with a milestone in offline learning services now contributing over 10% of total revenues. Management reiterated a strategic shift towards balanced growth and profitability, projecting Q4 revenue of RMB 1,628–1,648 million and expecting approximately 15% revenue growth in 2026, supported by disciplined cost control and a robust cash position. The company is executing its expanded share repurchase program while focusing on becoming a premium lifelong learning platform through technology-driven solutions and a diversified service model.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Nuvation Bio to discontinue development of NUV-1511
data.description:
Nuvation Bio (NUVB) has decided to halt the development of NUV-1511, the initial compound in its innovative drug-drug conjugate program. 

Although NUV-1511, under investigation for solid tumors, was generally well tolerated and showed some encouraging results in certain patients, the company concluded that the efficacy was not consistently strong across different groups. 

Nuvation Bio is not abandoning its drug-drug conjugate platform. Instead, the insights gained from the NUV-1511 program will be utilized to assess new candidates that may exhibit more reliable effectiveness. 

The funds initially allocated for NUV-1511, estimated at $100-150 million for research and development through 2029, will now be redirected towards other projects in the pipeline and the advancement of next-generation drug-drug conjugate candidates.

Additionally, the company will maintain its focus on the commercial launch of Ibtrozi (taletrectinib) and continue developing this program.

data.title: Li Auto outlines Q4 delivery target of up to 110,000 vehicles while returning to entrepreneurial management model
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Li Auto Inc. (LI) Q3 2025

Management View

  • Xiang Li, Founder, Executive Chairman & CEO, highlighted a major organizational shift stating, "starting from Q4 this year, I and my founding team will firmly revert back to the entrepreneurial model and to embrace the new era and new technological challenges." He emphasized the importance of increasing efficiency, focusing on user value, and solving key issues to thrive in a highly competitive and rapidly changing environment.

  • Li pointed to a strategic focus on developing embodied AI vehicles, describing the company's ambition to transform cars into proactive robots, which "can not only drive but also pick you up, park for you, have to charge the car up, have to close the door, open the door and meticulously make your life more convenient and safer."

  • Tie Li, CFO, stated, "Total revenues in the third quarter were RMB 27.4 billion, decreased 36.2% year-over-year and 9.5% quarter-over-quarter... Vehicle margin in the third quarter was 15.5% versus 20.9% in the same period last year and 19.4% in the prior quarter. The year-over-year decrease was mainly due to estimated Li MEGA recall cost and the higher per unit manufacturing cost from lower production volume."

Outlook

  • Tie Li reported, "For the fourth quarter of 2025, the company expects the deliveries to be between 100,000 and 110,000 vehicles and quarterly total revenues to be between RMB 36.5 billion (sic) [RMB 26.5 billion] and RMB 29.2 billion."

  • Management signaled that the business outlook reflects current and preliminary views on business and market conditions and is subject to change.

Financial Results

  • Tie Li disclosed, "Cost of sales in the third quarter was RMB 22.9 billion, down 32% year-over-year and 5.3% quarter-over-quarter. Gross profit in the third quarter was RMB 4.5 billion, down 51.6% year-over-year and 26.3% quarter-over-quarter."

  • Operating expenses were RMB 5.6 billion, with R&D expenses at RMB 3 billion, up 15% year-over-year. The sequential increase was mainly due to product configuration adjustment expenses.

  • Loss from operations was RMB 1.2 billion versus RMB 3.4 billion income from operations in the same period last year. Net loss in the third quarter was RMB 624.4 million compared to RMB 2.8 billion net income in the same period last year.

  • Free cash flow was negative RMB 8.9 billion in the third quarter versus RMB 9.1 billion in the same period last year and negative RMB 3.8 billion in the prior quarter.

Q&A

  • Yingbo Xu, Citic Securities: Asked about 2026 technology/product progress and BEV transition. Xiang Li responded, "next year, we'll be launching our AI system based on our internally developed M100 chips... our products would go from a passive... machine that passively takes orders to a more automated machine and even a proactive machine that can provide services for the users." On BEV, he detailed in-house developed electric drive, battery, and electronic control systems, with Li Auto’s 5C batteries entering mass production next year.

  • Tim Hsiao, Morgan Stanley: Asked about Li i8/i6 orders, supply bottlenecks, and cash flow trends. Xiang Li noted i-Series ramp-up with a dual-supplier strategy for Li i6 batteries, targeting monthly capacity of 20,000 units early next year. Tie Li attributed cash outflow to delivery pressure and a shortened supplier payment cycle mandated by government policy.

  • Ming-Hsun Lee, BofA: Queried impact of EV purchase tax/subsidy changes and 2026 model upgrades. Xiang Li outlined a peace of mind purchase program for i6 customers and projected NEV penetration in China to reach 55%-60% in 2026. He described a major generational upgrade for the L Series, with 5C supercharging standard on all models and a simplified SKU approach.

  • Paul Gong, UBS: Asked about MEGA recall booking and VLA AI model feedback. Tie Li explained the recall was accrued in Q3 as a subsequent event, impacting deliveries. Xiang Li noted positive user feedback on the VLA Driver and ongoing OTA updates, with new features and hardware integration planned by year-end.

  • Tina Hou, Goldman Sachs: Requested update on in-house SoC and OS. Tie Li highlighted their AI inference system, reduced development time, and cost savings, with the M100 chip undergoing large-scale testing and commercial development expected next year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was probing, with questions on supply chain, cash flow, product rollout speed, and policy risks. Concerns about cash outflows, delivery delays, and policy-driven demand shifts were recurrent.

  • Management acknowledged challenges directly but focused on long-term strategy and innovation. Xiang Li reiterated commitment to "embrace our beloved entrepreneurial management style and build leading body intelligence products."

  • Relative to the previous quarter, both analysts and management expressed more caution and urgency, with heightened attention to near-term operational and financial pressures.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management shifted from a professional management model to an entrepreneurial approach, signaling a significant change in organizational philosophy.

  • Guidance for Q4 deliveries (100,000-110,000 vehicles) was higher than Q3 actuals, but margins and profitability were notably weaker due to recall costs and lower production volumes.

  • Analysts in both quarters focused on sales, supply chain, and technology, but this quarter’s questions reflected deeper concern about execution risks and financial sustainability.

  • Management’s tone was more defensive and forward-looking, emphasizing innovation and organizational change as remedies for current setbacks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges cited included supply chain constraints, product lifecycle issues, policy changes, and the impact of recalls on margins and deliveries.

  • Management outlined mitigation via dual supplier strategies, in-house technology development, and localized supply chains.

  • Analyst concerns centered on cash outflows, delivery delays, and the industry’s shift from policy-driven to market-driven demand.

Final Takeaway

Li Auto’s third quarter marked a strategic reset as the company reaffirmed its entrepreneurial roots, prioritized embodied AI innovation, and set ambitious Q4 delivery targets. Despite short-term financial setbacks from recalls and supply chain disruptions, management expressed confidence in new product launches, proprietary AI hardware, and major upgrades planned for 2026, positioning the company for long-term leadership in intelligent vehicles.
